category_title: Pupils who act out due to frustration with learning tasks
minimal_context: The student is completing an independent test at their desk in the classroom.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please start working on your test now. You need to complete this independently.

STUDENT: (Looks at the paper) I don't really like the work, it's too hard.

TUTOR: Just keep working on it.

STUDENT: I've gubbed up [made a mess of it] already. Doing a test or something, I probably know I could have done better.

TUTOR: Try to figure it out on your own.

STUDENT: (Pushes the test away) I'll just carry on and then get into trouble. I get frustrated when I don't get help when I need it!

options.A: Check the student's progress on the testing platform every Sunday night to see what mistakes the student is making and what specific topics they are struggling with.
options.B: Organize a set of lessons to help the student become a 'Test Taking Superstar,' focusing on bubbling, reading directions, eliminating choices, double-checking, and the do’s and don’ts of test-taking.
options.C: Establish, through consensus, the working practices of the classroom via an SG Pledge and, thereafter, reinforce it; make clear the expectations held of the student and hold them to account.
options.D: Implement independent classroom activities and tests with less demanding rules and procedures for the student to follow during the session.
